name: Chinese Elm Bonsai and Care Kit in 18cm Navy Ceramic Pot
description: NEW STOCK RELEASE! Chinese Elm Broom Style - Zelkova bonsai tree, scissor/wire offer. Evergreen bonsai. Planted in a Blue ceramic pot. These Chinese elm bonsai trees can be grown indoors and outdoors. However, they do require winter protection and a small amount of maintenance to fully thrive. A very fast growing, forgiving variety. A great place to start and learn ancient art of bonsai The popular Chinese elm trees were one of the first bonsai trees to thrive in Europe from Asia. Photographs taken : November 2025 Evergreen Summer: cool indoor/outdoor Winter: will require frost protection Package includes: 1 - Chinese Elm Bonsai tree 1 - Budget bonsai scissors 1 - Aluminium training wire ( 1m x 1.5mm) 1 -Aluminium Branch training wire ( 1m x 2mm) 1 - Bonsai Feed ( one growing season)

name: NANBAN Round Ceramic Bonsai pot 17cm
description: The earliest forms of Nanban ceramics were introduced to Japan in the latter half of the 16th century from the south of China and Southeast Asia . The rustic charm of coil built Nanban ceramics makes them a perfect fit for contemporary interior design,draws on natural materials and textures to create calming, grounded environments. The wabi-sabi philosophy, which embraces imperfection, aligns well with modern minimalistic aesthetics, making Nanban pots ideal for spaces seeking to blend simplicity with profound artistic heritage. Our handcrafted ceramic pots are all designed and created by us at Zero Bonsai. Each pot having slightly different detail, size, features and colour variations. Created from wet clay, using techniques inspired by pot makers in Japan, carved out of wet clay, coil or slab built. All are high fired and glazed in a variety of colours. Perfect for that special tree or excellent as a gift. Ceramic : 17cm Misshapen Nanban Pot - Coil Built in our local DW Derwent Valley Clay by Zero Bonsai

name: Japanese Pepper Bonsai 'Broom Style' - 15cm Navy Ceramic Pot
description: The Chinese Pepper Bonsai (Zanthoxylum piperitum) is often kept as an indoor bonsai due to its preference for bright, indirect light and warm, stable temperatures, though it benefits greatly from being placed outdoors in a sunny, sheltered spot during the warm summer months. Consistent watering is crucial; allow the topsoil to become slightly dry between thorough waterings, but never let the root ball dry out completely, and aim to increase humidity with regular misting or a humidity tray. Fertilise with a balanced liquid bonsai food every 2-4 weeks during the active growing season (spring to autumn) Pot: 15cm Pot: rim to apex 15 cm Summer Outdoor Winter Cool Indoor location - Frost protection required

description: Berberis, or Barberry, in development as a bonsai. Excellent choice for bonsai due to its naturally small leaves, colourful foliage, and tendency to back-bud easily. name: Fukien Tea Tree Bonsai 'Shaped' - 15cm Cream Ceramic Pot
description: Indoor Bonsai Fukien Tea Tree (Carmona retusa) bonsai thrives in warm, tropical conditions, making it an excellent indoor bonsai. It requires bright, consistent light, ideally from a south or west-facing window, and must be protected from temperatures below 15∘C. Watering is critical: keep the soil consistently moist but never soggy, watering thoroughly when the top layer feels slightly dry, and increasing humidity with a humidity tray or regular misting is highly beneficial. Fertilise with a balanced bonsai food every 2-4 weeks during the active growing season to support its health, its glossy green leaves, and its delicate white flowers. Pot: 15cm Cream Ceramic Pot: rim to apex 20 cm

name: Hemlock - Tsuga STARTER Bonsai
description: Hemlock Bonsai (Tsuga) is a graceful evergreen conifer, highly valued for its fine, feathery foliage, delicate branching structure, and naturally weeping habit. While its general care is straightforward, requiring consistent moisture and protection from intense sun, it is generally considered an intermediate difficulty due to its sensitivity to drying out and the need for careful wiring to avoid marking its fast-swelling bark. Hemlock 4+ Year old STARTER - tsuga Note: Collection 2020 Latest Re-pot : Spring 2024 Photographs: November 2025 Currently in a Plastic Development Pot

With regular watering, sunlight, and occasional fertilisation, this bonsai thrives. Symbolically, it represents balance and endurance, reminding us of our own strength. name: NANBAN Round Ceramic Bonsai pot 18cm
description: The earliest forms of Nanban ceramics were introduced to Japan in the latter half of the 16th century from the south of China and Southeast Asia . The rustic charm of coil built Nanban ceramics makes them a perfect fit for contemporary interior design,draws on natural materials and textures to create calming, grounded environments. The wabi-sabi philosophy, which embraces imperfection, aligns well with modern minimalistic aesthetics, making Nanban pots ideal for spaces seeking to blend simplicity with profound artistic heritage. Our handcrafted ceramic pots are all designed and created by us at Zero Bonsai. Each pot having slightly different detail, size, features and colour variations. Created from wet clay, using techniques inspired by pot makers in Japan, carved out of wet clay, coil or slab built. All are high fired and glazed in a variety of colours. Perfect for that special tree or excellent as a gift. Ceramic : 18cm Nanban Pot - Coil Built and crafted in our local DW Derwent Valley Clay by Zero Bonsai

name: Chinese Box Bonsai Tree - Rock - 16cm Cream Ceramic Pot
description: The Boxwood Bonsai (Buxus harlandii) is a hardy, broadleaf evergreen that thrives in a bright, sheltered outdoor location, ideally receiving morning sun and afternoon shade, though it can tolerate full sun with consistent watering. Watering is key: allow the topsoil to dry out slightly before soaking thoroughly, as this species is susceptible to root rot if kept continually soggy. Though tolerant of cold, it requires protection from hard frost in winter to prevent root damage in the small pot. Fertilise regularly during the growing season (spring to autumn) with a balanced feed to support its dense, glossy green foliage, and prune often to maintain its compact shape. Pot: 16cm - Cream Import Pot: rim to apex 25cm
shortDescription: The Boxwood Bonsai (Buxus harlandii) is a hardy, broadleaf evergreen that thrives in a bright, sheltered outdoor location, ideally receiving morning sun and afternoon shade, though it can tolerate full sun with consistent watering. name: NANBAN Round Ceramic Bonsai pot 11cm
description: The earliest forms of Nanban ceramics were introduced to Japan in the latter half of the 16th century from the south of China and Southeast Asia . The rustic charm of coil built Nanban ceramics makes them a perfect fit for contemporary interior design,draws on natural materials and textures to create calming, grounded environments. The wabi-sabi philosophy, which embraces imperfection, aligns well with modern minimalistic aesthetics, making Nanban pots ideal for spaces seeking to blend simplicity with profound artistic heritage. Our handcrafted ceramic pots are all designed and created by us at Zero Bonsai. Each pot having slightly different detail, size, features and colour variations. Created from wet clay, using techniques inspired by pot makers in Japan, carved out of wet clay, coil or slab built. All are high fired and glazed in a variety of colours. Perfect for that special tree or excellent as a gift. Ceramic : 11cm Misshapen Nanban Pot - Coil Built in our local DW Derwent Valley Clay by Zero Bonsai

name: Japanese White Pine Bonsai
description: Imported Japanese White Pine - Pinus Parviflora The Japanese White Pine Bonsai (Pinus parviflora) is a highly revered evergreen conifer, treasured for its soft, five-needle foliage, beautiful bark, and the classic, aged elegance it brings to a collection. While physically hardy, the detailed, specialised techniques required for energy balancing (candle pinching/pruning) and its sensitivity to overwatering and poor drainage make it a species of intermediate to advanced difficulty. Photographs taken April 2025 Tree Size: rim to apex 40cm Vessel: Traditional Unglazed rectangular 28cm Pot - Imported

name: Berberis Starter Bonsai material
description: Berberis, or Barberry, in development as a bonsai. Excellent choice for bonsai due to its naturally small leaves, colourful foliage, and tendency to back-bud easily. Focus on using the "clip-and-grow" method to establish branch structure, as its wood can be brittle for wiring, and ensure it receives full sun to partial shade for the best autumn colour. name: Ryuga Wire / Fine Brach Scissors - Short Handle 115mm Stainless Steel
description: Ryuga, Wire/ Fine Branch Scissors - short handle, 115mm, stainless steel These wire cutters from Ryuga are confusingly similar to the premium tool from Japanese Masakuni. In comparison, however, it reaches its limit a little faster with thicker wire diameters, but it is also many times cheaper. Otherwise, it can be used like its Japanese model and therefore ideal for extensive and time-consuming wire work on pines and junipers. Due to the special design, you can easily hold these scissors in your hand while you wire a branch. Keep a finger in the handle so that the scissors do not slip out of your hand. In addition, smaller branches can be removed, making this tool very effective in bonsai care. However! for thicker wire, you should still cut with an ordinary wire cutter to avoid damaging this fine work tool.

name: NANBAN Round Ceramic Bonsai pot 17cm
description: The earliest forms of Nanban ceramics were introduced to Japan in the latter half of the 16th century from the south of China and Southeast Asia . The rustic charm of coil built Nanban ceramics makes them a perfect fit for contemporary interior design,draws on natural materials and textures to create calming, grounded environments. The wabi-sabi philosophy, which embraces imperfection, aligns well with modern minimalistic aesthetics, making Nanban pots ideal for spaces seeking to blend simplicity with profound artistic heritage. Our handcrafted ceramic pots are all designed and created by us at Zero Bonsai. Each pot having slightly different detail, size, features and colour variations. Created from wet clay, using techniques inspired by pot makers in Japan, carved out of wet clay, coil or slab built. All are high fired and glazed in a variety of colours. name: Trident Maple Bonsai
description: Trident Maple (Acer buergerianum) is highly valued for its fast growth, rapid root development, and ability to easily develop a thick, spreading root base (nebari), which is central to its design development. Care is straightforward due to its exceptional hardiness and resilience, making it a species where shaping is achieved quickly through heavy pruning, frequent repotting, and regular defoliation. 'This Trident has spent the last few years on a bench in our nursery! Deciduous Approximate Age 10+ years Repotted Spring 2025 PLEASE NOTE: Photographs Dec 2025 Ceramic L- 18cm W-15cm Unglazed Import Tree Height - Rim to apex 39cm

name: Japanese Black Pine Bonsai
description: Japanese Black Pine - Pinus thunbergii ' Kotobuki' The Japanese Black Pine Bonsai (Pinus thunbergii) is arguably the most iconic evergreen conifer in the art of bonsai, celebrated for its rugged, dark bark, stiff dark-green needles, and its ability to convey immense age and power. While it is a hardy, resilient outdoor tree, the highly specialised, twice-a-year pruning techniques required for de candling and balancing energy make it a species of advanced difficulty. Photographs taken April 2025 Tree Size: rim to apex 46cm Vessel: Traditional Unglazed Round 22cm Pot - Imported

name: Spruce Bonsai a Ceramic Ember Brown Abstract
description: Spruce Bonsai (Picea) is a classic evergreen conifer, recognised for its short, stiff needles that give the tree a dense appearance and its aged, dark-fissured bark. While hardy and tolerant of cold, the Spruce is considered an intermediate to advanced subject because it is slow to back-bud, requires precise seasonal needle and bud pruning for ramification, and dislikes being repotted. Partially styled 'collected' young Spruce Currently placed in a Ceramic Ember Brown Abstract Vessel by Zero Bonsai A great species to add to your Bonsai Collection Note: Repotted 2024

name: Japanese White Pine Bonsai
description: Japanese - White Pine - Pinus Parviflora The Japanese White Pine Bonsai (Pinus parviflora) is a highly revered evergreen conifer, treasured for its soft, five-needle foliage, beautiful bark, and the classic, aged elegance it brings to a collection. While physically hardy, the detailed, specialised techniques required for energy balancing (candle pinching/pruning) and its sensitivity to overwatering and poor drainage make it a species of intermediate to advanced difficulty. Photographs taken Jan 2025 Tree Size: rim to apex 36 cm Vessel: Traditional Size 6 Japanese Growing pot ( 20cm Tough Round)

name: Juniper - Japanese Imported Bonsai
description: Japanese import An excellent specimenThe Juniper Bonsai (Juniperus) is an incredibly popular evergreen conifer, highly valued for its dense, scale-like foliage and exceptional versatility for dramatic styling, including jin (deadwood) and shari (stripping bark). While Junipers are hardy outdoor trees that tolerate some watering neglect, they are often considered intermediate difficulty because they require consistent, bright outdoor sunlight, and improper pruning or letting the root ball dry out completely can easily lead to irreversible die-back. The tree listed and photographed is the tree you will receive Photographs taken June 2025 Ceramic: 22cm Light Matt Green Crackle Glaze Rectangular Dish - by Zero Bonsai Tree: 33 Rim to Apex

name: Deshojo Japanese Maple Bonsai+ Care Kit
description: Just Arrived !New Stock Release! Japanese Import! Japanese Deshojo Maple (Acer palmatum 'Deshojo') is specifically developed for its spectacular spring foliage, which emerges in brilliant scarlet before slowly changing to green for the summer. Care requires placing the tree in strong morning sun to maintain the intense spring colour, but shielding it from harsh afternoon sun which can scorch the delicate leaves, with careful, consistent watering being vital to its health. Re-potted 2025 Photographs taken Dec 2025 Ceramic: Blue 19cm Oval Dish - Import

name: Deodar Cedar Group Bonsai Planting
description: Deodar Cedar Bonsai (Cedrus deodara) is a magnificent, fast-growing evergreen conifer admired for its drooping, tiered branches and soft, short, blue-green to dark-green needles. As it is highly resilient, establishes quickly, and tolerates hard pruning when young, it is generally considered to have an easy to moderate difficulty level for a conifer. Cedar 3 Tree Planting NOTE: Photographs Taken Dec 25 Last Repot - Autumn 25 Ceramic: Venus Brown 22cm Rectangular shallow dish - by Zero Bonsai

name: Cotoneaster Bonsai Tree
description: Bonsai tree: 5+ Year old Cotoneaster - (white flowers, Red berries) Welcome to the world of Mame Bonsai pots, where artistry and nature intertwine on a miniature scale. Mame, derived from the Japanese word for "bean," aptly describes the tiny yet captivating universe of these bonsai containers. Note : Repotted 2024 There are many Cotoneaster bonsai about, a very interesting tree, white flowers, followed by orange berries. A bonsai species that is very forgiving and excellent for a novice to develop their bonsai skillsMust be kept outside, loves a sunny location and to be well watered Ceramic: 5cm Symphony Blue - White Stoneware Cube Pot - by Zero Bonsai

name: Chinese Elm Bonsai and Care Kit in 15cm Navy Ceramic Pot
description: Chinese Elm - Zelkova bonsai tree, scissor/wire offer. Semi evergreen bonsai. Planted in a blue ceramic pot. These Chinese elm bonsai trees can be grown indoors and outdoors. However, they do require winter protection and a small amount of maintenance to fully thrive. A very fast growing, forgiving variety. A great place to start and learn ancient art of bonsai The popular Chinese elm trees were one of the first bonsai trees to thrive in Europe from Asia. Semi Evergreen Summer: cool indoor/outdoor Winter: will require frost protection Package includes: 1 - Chinese Elm Bonsai tree 1 - Budget bonsai scissors 1 - Aluminium training wire ( 1m x 1.5mm) 1 -Aluminium Branch training wire ( 1m x 2mm) 1 - Bonsai Feed ( one growing season)

name: Deshojo Japanese Maple Bonsai+ Care Kit
description: Just Arrived! New Stock Release! Japanese Import! Japanese Deshojo Maple (Acer palmatum 'Deshojo') is specifically developed for its spectacular spring foliage, which emerges in brilliant scarlet before slowly changing to green for the summer. Care requires placing the tree in strong morning sun to maintain the intense spring colour, but shielding it from harsh afternoon sun which can scorch the delicate leaves, with careful, consistent watering being vital to its health. Re-potted 2025 Photographs taken Dec 2025 Ceramic: Olive 18cm Oval Dish - Import

name: Juniper -Juniperus communis - Bonsai - 28cm Abstract Ember Brown Vessel
description: Juniper bonsai- Juniperus communis green carpet semi styled The Juniper Bonsai (Juniperus) is an incredibly popular evergreen conifer, highly valued for its dense, scale-like foliage and exceptional versatility for dramatic styling, including jin (deadwood) and shari (stripping bark). While Junipers are hardy outdoor trees that tolerate some watering neglect, they are often considered intermediate difficulty because they require consistent, bright outdoor sunlight, and improper pruning or letting the root ball dry out completely can easily lead to irreversible die-back. Photographs taken December 2025 Ceramic: 28cm Ember Brown Abstract vessel - by Zero Bonsai

Mame, derived from the Japanese word for "bean," aptly describes the tiny yet captivating universe of these bonsai containers. Note : Repotted 2024 There are many Pyracantha bonsai about, a very interesting tree, white flowers, followed by berries. A bonsai species that is very forgiving and excellent for a novice to develop their bonsai skillsMust be kept outside, loves a sunny location and to be well watered Mame Ceramic: 6.5cm Oatmeal Cascade Cube Pot - by Zero Bonsai

As it is highly resilient, establishes quickly, and tolerates hard pruning when young, it is generally considered to have an easy to moderate difficulty level for a conifer. name: Chinese Box Bonsai Informal Style - 15cm Blue Ceramic Pot
description: The Boxwood Bonsai (Buxus harlandii) is a hardy, broadleaf evergreen that thrives in a bright, sheltered outdoor location, ideally receiving morning sun and afternoon shade, though it can tolerate full sun with consistent watering. Watering is key: allow the topsoil to dry out slightly before soaking thoroughly, as this species is susceptible to root rot if kept continually soggy. Though tolerant of cold, it requires protection from hard frost in winter to prevent root damage in the small pot. Fertilise regularly during the growing season (spring to autumn) with a balanced feed to support its dense, glossy green foliage, and prune often to maintain its compact shape. Pot: 15cm - Blue Import Pot: rim to apex 25cm
shortDescription: The Boxwood Bonsai (Buxus harlandii) is a hardy, broadleaf evergreen that thrives in a bright, sheltered outdoor location, ideally receiving morning sun and afternoon shade, though it can tolerate full sun with consistent watering. name: Bonsai Starter Bundle - Japanese Maple
description: Late Summer Maple SALE! Japanese Maple - Bonsai Starter Bundle Enter the ancient art of developing Real Trees in miniature The perfect way to enter the world of bonsai. This set includes everything you need to develop your own unique bonsai to enjoy for years to come. Note Photos Taken : Late Spring 2025 - Now experiencing Autumn Leaf Fall This starter bundle includes: 1x Tree - Japanese Maple 3+ year old starter tree ready to repot. Japanese Maple varieties currently include: Going Green, Golden Dream , Atropurpureum 1x Pot - 6.5cm bonsai CUBE pot - various colours stock as available - by Zero Bonsai 1x Soil - 200g Zero Bonsai soil mix. 2x Wire - Aluminium branch shaping wire 2mm & 1.5mm 1x Mesh - Drainage mesh. 1x Scissors - 105mm Basic Bonsai Scissors. 1 x Bio Gold - Japanese Bonsai Feed 50g You will also receive a link to our exclusive repotting video. It's perfect for beginners in the world of bonsai, allowing you to follow along step by step. About your Maple: Japanese Maple - Acer Palmatum The Japanese Maple Bonsai is a captivating plant with elegance, compact size, Elegant colourful leaves. It's easy to care for and resilient, making it perfect for bonsai beginners. The leaves create a stunning contrast against delicate branches as they change colour throughout the seasons. With regular watering, sunlight, and occasional fertilisation, this bonsai thrives. Symbolically, it represents balance and endurance, reminding us of our own strength. Choose a Japanese Maple for its beauty and easy care, bringing elegance to your home or garden.

name: Japanese White Pine Bonsai
description: Japanese - White Pine - Pinus Parviflora The Japanese White Pine Bonsai (Pinus parviflora) is a highly revered evergreen conifer, treasured for its soft, five-needle foliage, beautiful bark, and the classic, aged elegance it brings to a collection. While physically hardy, the detailed, specialised techniques required for energy balancing (candle pinching/pruning) and its sensitivity to overwatering and poor drainage make it a species of intermediate to advanced difficulty. Tree Size: rim to apex 35 cm This tree has recently received initial development styling Current photographs taken May 2025 Vessel: Venus Brown DEEP Handcrafted Bonsai Pot 18cm - by Zero Bonsai

name: Japanese Pepper Bonsai 'Broom Style' - 15cm Navy Ceramic Pot
description: The Chinese Pepper Bonsai (Zanthoxylum piperitum) is often kept as an indoor bonsai due to its preference for bright, indirect light and warm, stable temperatures, though it benefits greatly from being placed outdoors in a sunny, sheltered spot during the warm summer months. Consistent watering is crucial; allow the topsoil to become slightly dry between thorough waterings, but never let the root ball dry out completely, and aim to increase humidity with regular misting or a humidity tray. Fertilise with a balanced liquid bonsai food every 2-4 weeks during the active growing season (spring to autumn) Pot: 15cm Pot: rim to apex 15 cm Summer Outdoor Winter Cool Indoor location - Frost protection required

name: Cotoneaster Bonsai Tree
description: Bonsai tree: 5+ Year old Cotoneaster - (white flowers, Red berries) Welcome to the world of Mame Bonsai pots, where artistry and nature intertwine on a miniature scale. Mame, derived from the Japanese word for "bean," aptly describes the tiny yet captivating universe of these bonsai containers. Note : Repotted 2024 There are many Cotoneaster bonsai about, a very interesting tree, white flowers, followed by orange berries. A bonsai species that is very forgiving and excellent for a novice to develop their bonsai skillsMust be kept outside, loves a sunny location and to be well watered

name: Potentilla Flowering Bonsai
description: Potentilla Bonsai (Potentilla fruticosa pink ) is a charming deciduous shrub, esteemed for its fine branch structure, easily aged bark, and its long, colourful blooming period of small white, pink or yellow flowers. As it is extremely hardy, recovers quickly from heavy pruning, and readily back-buds, it is considered a robust subject with an easy to moderate difficulty level. NOTE: Photographs Taken Dec 25 Last Repot - 2025 Ceramic: Venus Brown 20cm DEEP Hexagon dish in Red Clay - by Zero Bonsai

name: Chinese Elm Bonsai in 20cm Topaz Ceramic Dish+ Care Kit
description: Enter the world on miniature bonsai. Chinese Elm - Zelkova bonsai tree planted in a Venus Brown ceramic pot Chinese elm bonsai trees can be grown indoors and outdoors. However, they do require winter protection and a small amount of maintenance to fully thrive. Very fast growing and forgiving. A great place to start and learn the ancient art of bonsai. Chinese elm were one of the first bonsai trees to arrive in Europe from Asia. Summer: cool indoor/outdoor Winter: requires frost protection Ceramic: 20 cm Topaz Rectangular Ceramic Dish - by Zero Bonsai Care Kit: Scissors, shaping wire and feed for one season

name: Japanese Pepper Bonsai S Trunk - 30cm Blue Ceramic Pot
description: The Chinese Pepper Bonsai (Zanthoxylum piperitum), often kept as an indoor bonsai due to its preference for bright, indirect light and warm, stable temperatures, though it benefits greatly from being placed outdoors in a sunny, sheltered spot during the warm summer months. Consistent watering is crucial; allow the topsoil to become slightly dry between thorough waterings, but never let the root ball dry out completely, and aim to increase humidity with regular misting or a humidity tray. Fertilise with a balanced liquid bonsai food every 2-4 weeks during the active growing season (spring to autumn) Pot: 30cm Pot: rim to apex 40 cm Summer Outdoor Winter Cool Indoor location - Frost protection required

name: Juniper - Japanese Imported Bonsai
description: Japanese import The Juniper Bonsai (Juniperus) is an incredibly popular evergreen conifer, highly valued for its dense, scale-like foliage and exceptional versatility for dramatic styling, including jin (deadwood) and shari (stripping bark). While Junipers are hardy outdoor trees that tolerate some watering neglect, they are often considered intermediate difficulty because they require consistent, bright outdoor sunlight, and improper pruning or letting the root ball dry out completely can easily lead to irreversible die-back. Photographs taken June 2025 Ceramic: 22cm Green Rectangular Imported Pot Tree: 30 Rim to Apex

name: Deshojo Japanese Maple Bonsai+ Care Kit
description: Just Arrived New Stock Release! Japanese Import! Japanese Deshojo Maple (Acer palmatum 'Deshojo') is specifically developed for its spectacular spring foliage, which emerges in brilliant scarlet before slowly changing to green for the summer. Care requires placing the tree in strong morning sun to maintain the intense spring colour, but shielding it from harsh afternoon sun which can scorch the delicate leaves, with careful, consistent watering being vital to its health. Re-potted 2025 Photographs taken Dec 2025 Ceramic: Green 20cm Oval Dish - Import

name: Bonsai Starter Bundle - Signature
description: Signature - Bonsai Starter Bundle The perfect way to get into the world of bonsai. This set includes everything you need to pot and develop your own unique bonsai to enjoy for years to come. This starter bundle includes: 1x Tree - Mandarin - Ligustrum 5+ year old starter tree ready to repot. 1x Pot - Handmade Bonsai Pots / Colours vary as available - By Zero Bonsai 1x Soil - 500g Zero Bonsai soil mix. 2x Wire - Aluminium branch shaping wire. 1x Mesh - Drainage mesh. 1x Scissors - 120mm bonsai scissors. 1x Chopstick - Hand crafted chopstick. You will also receive a link to our exclusive repotting video. It's perfect for beginners in the world of bonsai, allowing you to follow along step by step. About your Ligustrum: A Timeless Beauty in Miniature. This bonsai is marvellous with elegant foliage and a compact size. It needs minimal maintenance and thrives in different environments. Symbolising harmony and balance, the Mandarin Bonsai is perfect for all skill levels. The mandarin Bonsai's elegant foliage adds sophistication to any space. Its compact size makes it ideal for small gardens, balconies, or indoor spaces. Whether on a tabletop or as a centrepiece, this bonsai captivates with delicate beauty. Adaptable to various climates and lighting conditions, the Mandarin Bonsai is suitable for all growers. With minimal maintenance, including watering and pruning, it continues to showcase timeless beauty. Its low-maintenance nature allows enthusiasts to enjoy bonsai without feeling overwhelmed.

name: Field Maple 'Clump Style' Bonsai in a 20cm Matt Blue Hexagonal Ceramic Dish
description: Late Summer Maple SALE! Kabudachi Style Bonsai (Clump Style) Field Maple Acer Campestre - seedlings planted in traditional Japanese Kabudachi (clump style). In bonsai training over 3 years, placed into this ceramic dish - Spring 2024Note Note: Photos Taken : Late Spring 2025 - Now experiencing Autumn Leaf Fall More Info: Acer campestre or field maple is a native British species well suited to bonsai cultivation. It is a super tough tree that easily accepts bonsai training. The 5 lobed leaves become small once the tree begins to ramify well and the branch structure is very good, making a nice Winter silhouette. Field Maple is fully hardy and will stand full sun to partial shade. Superb pale yellow Autumn colour. An unusual group planting of small maples that has the potential to develop into something very unusual. Ceramic: 20cm Matt Blue Hexagonal Pot - by Zero Bonsai

name: Fukien Tea Tree Bonsai 'Broom Style' - 15cm Cream Ceramic Pot
description: Indoor Bonsai Fukien Tea Tree (Carmona retusa) bonsai thrives in warm, tropical conditions, making it an excellent indoor bonsai. It requires bright, consistent light, ideally from a south or west-facing window, and must be protected from temperatures below 15∘C. Watering is critical: keep the soil consistently moist but never soggy, watering thoroughly when the top layer feels slightly dry, and increasing humidity with a humidity tray or regular misting is highly beneficial. Fertilise with a balanced bonsai food every 2-4 weeks during the active growing season to support its health, its glossy green leaves, and its delicate white flowers. Pot: 15cm Cream Ceramic Pot: rim to apex 20 cm

name: Chinese Box Bonsai Tree Informal Style - 15cm Blue Ceramic Pot
description: The Boxwood Bonsai (Buxus harlandii) is a hardy, broadleaf evergreen that thrives in a bright, sheltered outdoor location, ideally receiving morning sun and afternoon shade, though it can tolerate full sun with consistent watering. Watering is key: allow the topsoil to dry out slightly before soaking thoroughly, as this species is susceptible to root rot if kept continually soggy. Though tolerant of cold, it requires protection from hard frost in winter to prevent root damage in the small pot. Fertilise regularly during the growing season (spring to autumn) with a balanced feed to support its dense, glossy green foliage, and prune often to maintain its compact shape. Pot: 15cm - Blue Import Pot: rim to apex 25cm
shortDescription: The Boxwood Bonsai (Buxus harlandii) is a hardy, broadleaf evergreen that thrives in a bright, sheltered outdoor location, ideally receiving morning sun and afternoon shade, though it can tolerate full sun with consistent watering. name: Mature Field Maple Bonsai
description: Mature Field maple bonsaiField Maple (Acer Campestre) Collected in Derbyshire 5 years ago - In Development as Bonsai sinceRe Potted 2025More Info:Acer campestre or field maple is a native British species well suited to bonsai cultivation. It is a super tough tree that easily accepts bonsai training. The 5 lobed leaves become small once the tree begins to ramify well and the branch structure is very good, making a nice winter silhouette. The tree is fully hardy and will stand full sun to partial shade. Superb pale yellow Autumn colour.An group planting of maples that have the potential to develop into a very collectable bonsaiCeramic: 28cm Cream/ Brown Oval Dish- by Zero Bonsai

name: Japanese Pepper Bonsai 'Broom Style' - 15cm Blue Ceramic Pot
description: The Chinese Pepper Bonsai (Zanthoxylum piperitum) is often kept as an indoor bonsai due to its preference for bright, indirect light and warm, stable temperatures, though it benefits greatly from being placed outdoors in a sunny, sheltered spot during the warm summer months. Consistent watering is crucial; allow the topsoil to become slightly dry between thorough waterings, but never let the root ball dry out completely, and aim to increase humidity with regular misting or a humidity tray. Fertilise with a balanced liquid bonsai food every 2-4 weeks during the active growing season (spring to autumn) Pot: 15cm Pot: rim to apex 15 cm Summer Outdoor Winter Cool Indoor location - Frost protection required
shortDescription: The Chinese Pepper Bonsai (Zanthoxylum piperitum) is often kept as an indoor bonsai due to its preference for bright, indirect light and warm, stable temperatures, though it benefits greatly from being placed outdoors in a sunny, sheltered spot during the warm summer months. name: Biogold Organic Bonsai Fertiliser - 200g Sealed Bag
description: Biogold organic fertiliser for Bonsai in a handy 200g Bag. This amount will last 6/12 months for one Bonsai tree. It can be used on both indoor and outdoor Bonsai trees. Note: Fertiliser cubes can be placed on the soil surface or diluted and used occasionally during the growing season in a scheduled watering program.

name: Japanese Black Pine Bonsai
description: Japanese Black Pine - Pinus thunbergii ' Kotobuki' The Japanese Black Pine Bonsai (Pinus thunbergii) is arguably the most iconic evergreen conifer in the art of bonsai, celebrated for its rugged, dark bark, stiff dark-green needles, and its ability to convey immense age and power. While it is a hardy, resilient outdoor tree, the highly specialised, twice-a-year pruning techniques required for de candling and balancing energy make it a species of advanced difficulty. Photographs taken June 2025 Tree Size: rim to apex 46cm Vessel: Traditional Unglazed Rectangular 23cm Pot - Imported

name: Chinese Elm Bonsai and Care Kit in 15cm Navy Ceramic Pot
description: NEW STOCK RELEASE! Chinese Elm - Zelkova bonsai tree, scissor/wire offer. Evergreen bonsai. Planted in a blue ceramic pot. These Chinese elm bonsai trees can be grown indoors and outdoors. However, they do require winter protection and a small amount of maintenance to fully thrive. A very fast growing, forgiving variety. A great place to start and learn ancient art of bonsai The popular Chinese elm trees were one of the first bonsai trees to thrive in Europe from Asia. Photographs taken : November 2025 Evergreen Summer: cool indoor/outdoor Winter: will require frost protection Package includes: 1 - Chinese Elm Bonsai tree 1 - Budget bonsai scissors 1 - Aluminium training wire ( 1m x 1.5mm) 1 -Aluminium Branch training wire ( 1m x 2mm) 1 - Bonsai Feed ( one growing season)

However, they do require winter protection and a small amount of maintenance to fully thrive. Very fast growing and forgiving. A great place to start and learn the ancient art of bonsai. Chinese elm were one of the first bonsai trees to arrive in Europe from Asia. Summer: cool indoor/outdoor Winter: requires frost protection Ceramic: 35 cm Yellow Ying Chinese Pot

name: Deshojo Japanese Maple Bonsai+ Care Kit
description: Just Arrived - New Stock Release! Japanese Import! Japanese Deshojo Maple (Acer palmatum 'Deshojo') is specifically developed for its spectacular spring foliage, which emerges in brilliant scarlet before slowly changing to green for the summer. Care requires placing the tree in strong morning sun to maintain the intense spring colour, but shielding it from harsh afternoon sun which can scorch the delicate leaves, with careful, consistent watering being vital to its health. Re-potted 2025 Photographs taken Dec 2025 Ceramic: Olive 18cm Oval Dish - Import

Created from wet clay, using techniques inspired by pot makers in Japan, carved out of wet clay or slab built. All are high fired and glazed in a variety of colours. name: Large Chinese Elm Bonsai and Care Kit in 28cm Cream Ceramic Pot
description: NEW STOCK RELEASE! Chinese Elm - Zelkova bonsai tree, scissor/wire offer. Semi evergreen bonsai. Planted in a Cream ceramic pot. These Chinese elm bonsai trees can be grown indoors and outdoors. However, they do require winter protection and a small amount of maintenance to fully thrive. A very fast growing, forgiving variety. A great place to start and learn ancient art of bonsai The popular Chinese elm trees were one of the first bonsai trees to thrive in Europe from Asia. Photographs taken : Oct25 Semi Evergreen Summer: cool indoor/outdoor Winter: will require frost protection Package includes: 1 - Chinese Elm Bonsai tree 1 - Budget bonsai scissors 1 - Aluminium training wire ( 1m x 1.5mm) 1 -Aluminium Branch training wire ( 1m x 2mm) 1 - Bonsai Feed ( one growing season)
